mnemonics: NEW: Needed, Empty, Waiting - Remember that 'new' is needed to allocate memory, it creates an empty object, and it's waiting for the constructor
keyPoints: Declaration creates a reference variable, not an actual object
The 'new' keyword is required to instantiate/create the actual object
Object reference variables have a default value of 'null' until assigned
commonMistakes: Trying to use an object before instantiating it with 'new' (NullPointerException)
Confusing object declaration with instantiation - MyClass obj; vs MyClass obj = new MyClass();
Assuming primitive variable rules apply to object references (e.g., expecting default values other than null)
